title: The metafictional purpose of the 14 year timeskip
display_title: The metafictional purpose of the 14 year timeskip
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] The metafictional purpose of the 14 year timeskip             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri May 03, 2013 4:12 am ("14 year timeskip" is what is blanked out in the title for spoiler-proofing purposes.) I had a thought recently... Does it occur to anyone that perhaps the true, metafictional purpose of the timeskip is to account for the amount of time that has passed in the real world since NGE's airing? So that, e.g., the pilots are all now technically the age of those of us who would have been the target age of the original airing, and all the other characters have also aged to what they would be if they were real and had continued to age past the original airing of NGE? (And maybe also so that we who were fans of Asuka back when we were her age can now lust after her teenage body while having her be technically our age so that it's "okay". I used to joke that "It's not my fault that I grew up and she didn't," and now she has! ) 1997 + 14 = 2011. Not too far off, and maybe they would have hit it if not for the scrap-and-redo of 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Fri May 03, 2013 4:40 am This is my thinking to some degree. 3.33 seems to be some kind of comment on the original Eva (One idea I've had is that Shinji still functions as an audience surrogate in Rebuild, but representing something else: a kind of person that doesn't grow up and doesn't learn their lesson, and only continues to destroy and hurt the ones around them that they love, as represented by the destroyed world of 3.33 (Perhaps representing how the original Eva itself has been destroyed in some ways?)) or modern anime in general or something along those lines, and I can't quite put my finger on specifically what it is. Perhaps you're right and the reason that the pilots aren't aging is because the fans don't want them to. The "curse of the Eva" is actually the curse of being in a famous television series, and having to conform to audience's expectations. :P

[]:             Quote Post by AlphaGamma » Fri May 03, 2013 5:18 am The Killer of Heroes wrote: This is my thinking to some degree. 3.33 seems to be some kind of comment on the original Eva (One idea I've had is that Shinji still functions as an audience surrogate in Rebuild, but representing something else: a kind of person that doesn't grow up and doesn't learn their lesson, and only continues to destroy and hurt the ones around them that they love, as represented by the destroyed world of 3.33 (Perhaps representing how the original Eva itself has been destroyed in some ways?)) or modern anime in general or something along those lines, and I can't quite put my finger on specifically what it is. Perhaps you're right and the reason that the pilots aren't aging is because the fans don't want them to. The "curse of the Eva" is actually the curse of being in a famous television series, and having to conform to audience's expectations. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri May 03, 2013 10:23 am It allows Anno to go with whatever direction in the story he feels will evoke the biggest emotional response from the audience, without having to worry about setting it up before hand.
